title_en: Method and Approach: Brevity as a Scholarly Virtue
content_en:
The defining characteristic of Tafsir al-Jalalain is its method of conciseness (al-ikhtishar), and understanding this method is essential to using the work correctly. The two Jalals did not write a commentary intended to be read in isolation. They wrote a pedagogical instrument — a text designed to be studied alongside the Quran itself, line by line, with a teacher who can situate each gloss within the broader tradition.

The methodology proceeds through several consistent operations. First, lexical glossing: when a word in the Quran carries a specific meaning that a student might misread, the commentary supplies a synonym or brief definition. The Arabic phrase 'ay' (meaning 'that is') or 'wa huwa' (meaning 'which is') signals these glosses. A student learns to read through the Arabic Quran text and the interspersed commentary as a single flowing sentence.

Second, grammatical clarification: the Quran's Arabic employs ellipsis, implicit subjects, omitted objects, and pronoun references that can be ambiguous even for fluent Arabic speakers. Al-Jalalain consistently supplies what has been omitted. If a verb has an implied object, the commentary states it explicitly. If a pronoun could refer to two possible antecedents, the commentary specifies which one is intended in the preferred reading.

Third, occasion of revelation (sabab al-nuzul): for many verses, there is a specific historical event or question that occasioned their revelation. Al-Jalalain notes these briefly — sometimes in a single clause — without narrating full chains of transmission or adjudicating between competing reports. This gives the student a basic historical anchor without the complexity of the full hadith literature on the topic.

Fourth, variant readings (qira'at): the Quran was revealed in multiple authentic recitations. Al-Jalalain occasionally notes variant readings where they affect meaning, typically introduced with phrases like 'in one reading' or 'also read as.' This is selective rather than comprehensive — the work is not a qira'at manual, but students encounter the most significant variants.

The theological position of Tafsir al-Jalalain is broadly Ash'ari in its approach to divine attributes — the same Ash'ari Sunni tradition to which both al-Mahalli and al-Suyuti belonged. This is consistent with the dominant theological school of the Shafi'i tradition in which both scholars operated. Readers from other theological traditions within Ahl al-Sunnah — Athari or Maturidi — will find the commentary generally non-polemical and focused on linguistic and contextual matters rather than extended theological argument.

For advanced students, the commentary serves as a point of departure. When al-Jalalain notes that a word means X, the student learns to ask: what are the other positions? When it supplies an occasion of revelation, the student learns to consult the full isnad literature. The brevity of the commentary trains habits of question and investigation rather than passive reception. In this sense, its pedagogical value extends beyond the content it provides to the interpretive habits it cultivates.
title_id: Metode dan Pendekatan: Keringkasan sebagai Kebajikan Keilmuan
content_id:
Karakteristik utama Tafsir al-Jalalain adalah metode keringkasannya (al-ikhtishar), dan memahami metode ini sangat penting untuk menggunakan karya ini dengan benar. Kedua Jalal tidak menulis komentar yang dimaksudkan untuk dibaca secara mandiri. Mereka menulis instrumen pedagogis — sebuah teks yang dirancang untuk dipelajari berdampingan dengan Al-Qur'an itu sendiri, baris demi baris, dengan seorang guru yang dapat menempatkan setiap gloss dalam tradisi yang lebih luas.

Metodologi ini berlangsung melalui beberapa operasi yang konsisten. Pertama, penjabaran leksikal: ketika sebuah kata dalam Al-Qur'an memiliki makna spesifik yang mungkin disalahpahami oleh seorang pelajar — atau ketika ia memiliki beberapa makna yang mungkin dan konteks menentukan mana yang berlaku — kedua Jalal mengidentifikasi makna yang relevan secara singkat. Penjelasan ini bukan sebuah ceramah bahasa Arab; ia adalah sinyal langsung kepada pelajar tentang bagaimana membaca kata ini dalam ayat ini.

Kedua, identifikasi referensi: ketika sebuah ayat merujuk pada orang, tempat, peristiwa, atau konteks sejarah tertentu, kedua Jalal mengidentifikasinya dalam kata-kata sesedikit mungkin. Mereka tidak memberikan sejarah lengkap tentang suatu kisah; mereka mengidentifikasi kunci yang membuka ayat tersebut. Untuk ayat-ayat seperti "Dan orang yang mengikat kontrak..." (al-Ma'idah: 1), identifikasi ini sangat penting — tanpanya, teks tetap abstrak.

Ketiga, klarifikasi gramatikal: bahasa Arab klasik memiliki struktur gramatikal yang kaya di mana penempatan kata, perubahan kasus, dan pemilihan kala kerja semuanya membawa makna. Ketika pilihan gramatikal dalam sebuah ayat mempengaruhi cara ia harus dipahami, kedua Jalal menunjukkannya dengan tepat. Ini bukan pelajaran tata bahasa untuk pemula; ini adalah sinyal untuk pelajar yang sudah mengetahui tata bahasa dasar bahwa ada pertanyaan gramatikal yang perlu diperhatikan di sini.

Apa yang tidak ada dalam Jalalain sama pentingnya dengan apa yang ada. Tidak ada diskusi panjang tentang varian pendapat — pilihan metodologis antara tafsir bi al-ma'tsur (berdasarkan riwayat) dan tafsir bi al-ra'y (berdasarkan pendapat). Tidak ada pembahasan panjang tentang pertanyaan kalam (teologi spekulatif). Tidak ada eksplorasi hukum yang terperinci kecuali ayat tersebut secara langsung menetapkan hukum. Ringkasan mengharuskan keputusan tentang apa yang esensial, dan kedua Jalal membuat keputusan-keputusan tersebut dengan presisi yang luar biasa.

Nilai pedagogis dari metode ini sangat besar. Seorang pelajar yang membaca melalui Al-Qur'an dengan Jalalain di tangannya mendapatkan akses langsung ke apa yang ia butuhkan untuk memahami teks tanpa harus menyaring volume yang besar. Pada saat yang sama, kekakuan metode menjadikan batas-batas Jalalain jelas: ini bukan tafsir untuk memahami kedalaman penuh Al-Qur'an. Ia adalah teks yang mempersiapkan pelajar untuk tafsir yang lebih mendalam — al-Baghawi, Ibn Katsir, al-Qurthubi — dengan memastikan bahwa mereka datang ke teks-teks itu dengan pemahaman linguistik yang kokoh.
content_ar: The defining characteristic of التفسير al-Jalalain is its method of conciseness (al-ikhtishar), and الفهم this method is الجوهري to using the work correctly. The two Jalals did not write a commentary intended to be read in isolation. They wrote a pedagogical instrument — a text designed to be studied alongside the القرآن الكريم itself, line by line, with a teacher who can situate each gloss within the broader التراث. The methodology proceeds through several consistent operations. أولاً, lexical glossing: عندما a word in the القرآن الكريم carries a specific meaning that a student might misread, the commentary supplies a synonym or brief definition. The Arabic phrase 'ay' (meaning 'that is') or 'wa huwa' (meaning 'which is') signals these glosses. A student learns to read through the Arabic القرآن الكريم text and the interspersed commentary as a single flowing sentence. ثانياً, grammatical clarification: the القرآن الكريم's Arabic employs ellipsis, implicit subjects, omitted objects, and pronoun references that can be ambiguous even for fluent Arabic speakers. Al-Jalalain consistently supplies what has been omitted. إذا a verb has an implied object, the commentary states it explicitly. إذا a pronoun could refer to two possible antecedents, the commentary specifies which one is intended in the preferred reading. ثالثاً, occasion of الوحي (sabab al-nuzul): for many الآيات, there is a specific historical event or question that occasioned their الوحي. Al-Jalalain notes these briefly — sometimes in a single clause — without narrating full chains of transmission or adjudicating between competing reports. This gives the student a الأساسي historical anchor without the complexity of the full الحديث literature on the topic. رابعاً, variant readings (qira'at): the القرآن الكريم was revealed in multiple authentic recitations. Al-Jalalain occasionally notes variant readings where they affect meaning, typically introduced with phrases like 'in one reading' or 'أيضاً read as.' This is selective rather than comprehensive — the work is not a qira'at manual, but students encounter the most المهم variants. The theological position of التفسير al-Jalalain is broadly الأشعري in its approach to divine attributes — the same الأشعري Sunni التراث to which both al-Mahalli and al-Suyuti belonged. This is consistent with the dominant theological school of the الشافعي التراث in which both العلماء operated. Readers from other theological traditions within Ahl al-السنة — الأثري or الماتريدي — will find the commentary generally non-polemical and focused on linguistic and contextual matters rather than extended theological argument. For advanced students, the commentary serves as a point of departure. عندما al-Jalalain notes that a word means X, the student learns to ask: what are the other positions? عندما it supplies an occasion of الوحي, the student learns to consult the full الإسناد literature.
